title: Tensor network study of deconfined quantum criticality in a one-dimensional spin-phonon model
authors: Anton Romen, Josef Willsher, David Hofmeier, Johannes Knolle, Michael Knap
abstract: Deconfined quantum critical points (DQCPs) describe continuous transitions between ordered phases beyond the Landau paradigm. A simple example is the Néel antiferromagnet (AFM) to valence bond solid (VBS) transition in a 1D antiferromagnetic $J_1-J_2$ model. In analogy to the spin-Peierls instability of critical spin chains, DQCPs are predicted to be unstable towards lattice distortions below a critical phonon frequency. In this work, we use tensor network simulations to investigate this instability in the antiferromagnetic $J_1-J_2$ model coupled to lattice vibrations. We confirm the stability of DQCP for large phonon frequencies and demonstrate that the transition turns strongly first-order below a critical frequency. The instability is caused by a reduction of the Luttinger parameter due to spin-phonon interactions and we identify the effective theory of the behavior as the double sine-Gordon model. The same effective theory is known to describe the classical Ashkin-Teller model, which enables us to show that the critical endpoint is in the four-state Potts universality class. Furthermore, we provide quantitative numerical scaling results for the phonon spectral function, offering an experimental signature to probe DQCP-phonon coupling in low-dimensional materials.
summary: The paper uses tensor network simulations to study DQCP in a 1D spin-phonon model. It finds that while the critical point exists at high phonon frequencies, coupling to lattice vibrations drives the transition into a strongly first-order regime below a critical frequency. This work provides quantitative signatures like spectral function anomalies for experimental detection.
main_problem: The study investigates how lattice vibrations (phonons) affect Deconfined Quantum Critical Points (DQCPs) in one-dimensional spin systems, determining if the transition remains continuous or becomes first-order.
main_result: The DQCP persists for high phonon frequencies but turns strongly first-order below a critical frequency ($\omega_c$), leading to an effective description by the double sine-Gordon model.
method: Tensor network simulations (iDMRG/MPS) and bosonization techniques are employed across various limits, including static and dynamical spin-phonon coupling.
model_or_system: The system is a 1D antiferromagnetic $J_1-J_2$ spin chain coupled to lattice vibrations via a spin-phonon interaction term ($H_{sp}$). The low-energy physics is mapped onto the double sine-Gordon model.
key_observables: Luttinger parameter (K), order parameters (Néel/VBS), phonon spectral function $A(q, \omega)$, and Binder cumulant ($U_B$).
important_parameters: Phonon frequency ($\omega_0$), critical frequency ($\omega_c$), coupling constants ($J_1, J_2$), and the effective coupling strength $ho$.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on mapping to continuum theories (sine-Gordon) and using tensor network methods with finite bond dimensions ($\chi$) for numerical calculations.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the phase diagram showing DQCP above $\omega_c$ terminating at a Potts endpoint, and demonstrating the transition changing from continuous (power-law decay) to strongly first-order (discontinuous jumps in order parameters).
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes the spin model via bosonization, then incorporates phonon coupling by examining static ($\omega_0 	o 0$) vs. dynamical ($\omega_0 > 0$) limits, culminating in quantitative measurements of critical exponents and spectral functions.
why_interesting: This work provides a concrete example of how lattice degrees of freedom can fundamentally alter the nature of quantum phase transitions predicted by purely electronic models, which is crucial for understanding real materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Phase Diagram of a frustrated 1D spin-chain coupled to quantum phonons: a) We consider a 1D spin chain with antiferromagnetic nearest and next-nearest neighbor interactions and strong spin anisotropy ∆= 4. Spins are coupled to lattice distortions, modeled by site phonons with displacement uj at a single variable frequency ω0. b) Above the critical frequency ωc, the spin-phonon model realizes a line of deconfined quantum critical points with varying critical exponents between an Ising-N´eel ordered phase and a dimerized valence bond solid. The critical line ends at ωc, marked by an endpoint in the four-state Potts universality class. Below ωc the transition turns strongly...

FIG. 2. Summary of Deconfined Criticality in a 1D spin chain: a) Upon tuning J2/J1, the model undergoes a continuous transition from a gapped phase with N´eel or- der to a dimerized VBS phase. Numerical data displays a weakly first-order transition with length scale set by the MPS correlation length ξ. Upon increasing the bond dimen- sion χ, order parameter residuals at the transition vanish as ˆOJ2c ∼ξ(χ)−β/ν ∼ξ−K (inset), indicating a continuous tran- sition in the thermodynamic limit, see main text for details. b) Order parameter correlations at the transitions decay al- gebraically, with exponent set by the Luttinger parameter K of the critical theory.

FIG. 3. Spin-Peierls Instability of DQC in a 1D spin chain: a) Static lattice distortions drive the DQCP to a strong first-order transition, displayed by a clear jump in the order parameter. b) Inside the VBS phase, the system favours a sudden distortion of the underlying lattice δ ̸= 0 to lower the ground state energy. c) The lattice instability is captured by a sudden jump of the optimal lattice distortion δopt at the transition. d) The correlation length displays clear first-order character.

FIG. 4. DQCP in the spin-phonon model: a) When coupling to dynamical Einstein phonons with frequency ω0, the con- tinuous transition between N´eel and VBS phase persists above a critical phonon frequency ωc. b) Order parameter correlations at the transition decay algebraically, with power law exponent −2K governed by the Luttinger parameter of the critical theory. c) The order parameters develop a finite residual at the critical point, which vanishes with increasing correlation length ξ of the MPS ground state. The residuals are controlled using a sweep protocol initialized in one of the two ordered phases. Finite-size scaling predicts a power-law decay ˆOJ2c ∼ξ−β/ν = ξ−K, which we use to...

FIG. 5. Strong first-order transition in the spin- phonon model: Below a critical phonon frequency ωc, the transition between N´eel and VBS phase turns strongly first order. a) This is indicated by a clear jump in the order pa- rameters, b) rapid saturation of the correlation length, and c) a discontinuity in the energy derivative at the transition. d) Further evidence for a strong-first order transition is ob- tained from the VBS Binder Cumulant UB, which develops a diverging negative cusp with increasing system size. This behavior arises from a multi-peak distribution of the order pa- rameter in the presence of phase coexistence. Data obtained for ∆1 = ∆2 = 4, g = 0.25J1, ω0 = 0.4J1.
